question_text: A spanner is used to tighten a bolt. Which change would make it easier to turn the bolt: using a longer spanner or applying the same force closer to the bolt?

options.label: A
options.text: Using a longer spanner would make it easier

options.label: B
options.text: Applying force closer to the bolt would make it easier

options.label: C
options.text: Both changes would have the same effect

options.label: D
options.text: Neither change would affect how easy it is to turn

correct_answer: Using a longer spanner would make it easier
explanation: A longer spanner increases the perpendicular distance from the pivot point (the bolt). Since moment = force × perpendicular distance, a larger distance means a larger turning effect (moment) is produced. This makes it easier to turn the bolt with the same applied force.
